title: Oh Canada, It's Party Time!
story: Red and white flags wave everywhere across Canada on July 1 as the whole country celebrates Canada Day! People gather in parks for massive barbecues, watch incredible fireworks light up the night sky, and cheer at parades filled with marching bands and colorful floats. Many Canadians wear red maple leaf shirts or paint their faces with the national colors. From coast to coast, communities host festivals with live music, games, and delicious treats like butter tarts and poutine to mark their nation's birthday.
activity_question: What colors do many Canadians wear to celebrate Canada Day?
bonus_fact: Canada Day fireworks can be seen from space on satellite images!

title: International Joke Day: Laugh Out Loud!
story: Every July 1, people around the world celebrate International Joke Day by sharing their funniest jokes, puns, and silly stories! Friends text each other corny dad jokes, families have joke-telling contests at dinner, and social media fills up with hilarious memes and one-liners. Comedy clubs host special shows, and even serious news websites post funny content for the day. Kids love sharing knock-knock jokes at school, while adults groan at terrible puns that are so bad they're actually good. It's the perfect excuse to be goofy and spread laughter everywhere you go!
activity_question: What do people commonly share on social media during International Joke Day?
follow_up_question: What's the funniest joke you know that always makes people laugh?

title: Mail Heroes Get Their Day!
story: Rain or shine, postal workers deliver millions of letters and packages to your door every single day! On July 1, National Postal Worker Day celebrates these hardworking heroes who make sure your birthday cards, online orders, and important mail reach you safely. People thank their mail carriers with cold drinks, snacks, or thank-you notes to show appreciation for their dedication.
activity_question: What do people give postal workers on National Postal Worker Day to show appreciation?
follow_up_question: What would happen if postal workers stopped delivering mail for just one week?

title: Scooping Up Wild and Wacky Flavors
story: Pizza-flavored ice cream, anyone? On July 1, National Creative Ice Cream Flavors Day celebrates the weirdest, wildest, and most wonderful frozen treats you can imagine! Ice cream shops across the country go crazy with unusual combinations like bacon maple, lavender honey, or even pickle ice cream. Some brave taste-testers try flavors made with hot sauce, cheese, or vegetables. Food bloggers post photos of the strangest scoops they can find, while families challenge each other to invent new flavor ideas at home. It's the perfect day to step outside your vanilla comfort zone and discover that ice cream can taste like almost anything!
activity_question: What do ice cream shops do on National Creative Ice Cream Flavors Day?
activity_choices: Go crazy with unusual flavor combinations
activity_answer: Go crazy with unusual flavor combinations
bonus_fact: Some ice cream makers have created flavors using insects, soap, and even dirt!

title: Snap, Crackle, and Gingersnap!
story: Crunchy gingersnap cookies take center stage every July 1 on National Gingersnap Day! These spicy-sweet treats get their kick from ginger, cinnamon, and molasses. People celebrate by baking fresh batches, dunking them in milk, or crushing them up for pie crusts. Some families have gingersnap baking contests to see whose cookies have the loudest snap when broken in half. Coffee shops and bakeries often feature special gingersnap flavors like ice cream or lattes to mark the day.
activity_question: What spices give gingersnap cookies their special flavor?
bonus_fact: Gingersnaps were once used as teething cookies for babies because they're so hard!

title: Stuffed Guardians Stand Tall Today
story: Grab some old clothes and start stuffing because July 1 is National Build a Scarecrow Day! Families across the country head to their backyards, gardens, and farms to create these straw-filled guardians. You can use anything from old jeans and flannel shirts to colorful dresses and funny hats. The best part is giving your scarecrow a personality with a painted face, wild hair made from yarn, or even sunglasses and a silly grin. Some people make traditional farm scarecrows to protect their vegetables, while others get creative with superhero costumes or sports jerseys. Communities often hold scarecrow-building contests where the wackiest designs win prizes!
activity_question: What do people commonly use to give their scarecrows personality?
bonus_fact: Some scarecrows are so realistic that birds actually avoid them for months!

title: Zip It Up for Zip Code Day!
story: Every July 1, people celebrate National Zip Code Day by appreciating those five magical numbers that help mail find its way! Post offices get extra busy as people mail letters and packages, making sure to write their zip codes clearly. Some families turn it into a game, seeing who can remember the most zip codes from places they've visited. Schools and libraries often host activities where kids learn how zip codes work like a secret address system. Social media fills with people sharing fun facts about their hometown zip codes and posting pictures of colorful mailboxes.
activity_question: What do people make sure to write clearly on July 1st?
bonus_fact: The first zip code ever used was 00601 in Puerto Rico!
